proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 5.408 (palbociclib=1, n=17428) vs 4.310 (=0, n=32572); Welch t-test p<1e-300.
Mean pfs 4.645 (tras=1, n=10039) vs 4.705 (=0); difference -0.060, p=0.029. Trastuzumab is associated with slightly SHORTER PFS unadjusted.
Mean pfs 4.660 (ola=1) vs 4.696 (=0); diff -0.036, p=0.33. No main effect.
Mean pfs 4.686 vs 4.694; diff -0.007, p=0.85. No main effect.
Mean pfs 4.630 (pembro=1, n=7585) vs 4.704 (=0); diff -0.074, p=0.018. Pembrolizumab unadjusted is associated with slightly SHORTER PFS.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_tamoxifen + treatment_palbociclib + treatment_trastuzumab + treatment_olaparib + treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan + treatment_pembrolizumab + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + tumor_size_cm + ki67_pct', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS: palbociclib coef=+1.103 (p<1e-300); pembrolizumab coef=-0.033 (p=0.038); olaparib coef=-0.034 (p=0.074); tamoxifen, trastuzumab, sacituzumab not significant. Confirms palbociclib has the only sizeable positive treatment main effect; pembrolizumab adjusted effect is mildly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ER-positive (er_positive=1) patients have longer pfs_months than ER-negative patients.
HER2-positive (her2_positive=1) patients have shorter pfs_months than HER2-negative patients.
PIK3CA-mutated (pik3ca_mutation=1) patients have shorter pfs_months than wild-type patients.
BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation carriers have shorter pfs_months than non-carriers.
HER2-low (her2_low=1) patients have different pfs_months than HER2-low-negative patients.
Node-positive patients have shorter pfs_months than node-negative patients.

analyses.result_summary: PFS mean 4.865 (ER+) vs 4.297 (ER-); diff +0.568, p=3.7e-143. Strongly supported.
PFS mean 4.317 (HER2+) vs 4.776 (HER2-); diff -0.459, p=6.1e-70. Supported.
PFS mean 4.328 (PIK3CA mut) vs 4.887 (wt); diff -0.559, p=6.1e-144. Strongly supported.

analyses.code: ttest by brca1_mutation; ttest by brca2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: BRCA1 diff -0.025 (p=0.72); BRCA2 diff -0.127 (p=0.08). No clear unadjusted effect.
PFS mean 4.765 (HER2-low) vs 4.650; diff +0.114, p=1.0e-6. Small positive association.
PFS mean 4.681 (node+) vs 4.705; diff -0.024, p=0.29. No effect detected.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ er_positive + pr_positive + her2_positive + her2_low + brca1_mutation + brca2_mutation + pik3ca_mutation + tp53_mutation + pten_loss + msi_high + node_positive + postmenopausal + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted: ER+ +0.543 (p=3e-211); HER2+ -0.464 (p=1e-171); PIK3CA -0.590 (p<1e-300). PR+, HER2-low, BRCA1/2, TP53, PTEN, MSI, node+, postmenopausal not significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months more in er_positive patients than in er_negative patients (positive treatment_tamoxifen x er_positive interaction on pfs_months).
treatment_trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months more in her2_positive patients than in her2_negative patients (positive treatment_trastuzumab x her2_positive interaction).
treatment_olaparib prolongs pfs_months more in BRCA1/2 mutation carriers than in non-carriers (positive treatment_olaparib x brca_any interaction).
treatment_palbociclib prolongs pfs_months more in ER+/HER2- patients than in others (positive treatment_palbociclib x er_pos_her2_neg interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months more in MSI-high patients than in MSI-low patients (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction).
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan prolongs pfs_months more in triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-) patients than in others.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ tamoxifen * er_positive + age + ecog + stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.009, p=0.77. No evidence that tamoxifen helps ER+ patients differentially.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ trastuzumab * her2_positive + age + ecog + stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.012, p=0.78. No evidence trastuzumab benefits HER2+ patients differentially.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ olaparib * brca_any + age + ecog + stage_iv (brca_any = brca1|brca2)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.140, p=0.16. Direction-consistent with PARP-inhibitor mechanism but not statistically significant.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ palbociclib * er_pos_her2_neg + age + ecog + stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +1.935, p<1e-300. Massive positive interaction. Palbociclib's main effect is essentially restricted to ER+/HER2- patients, mirroring the canonical CDK4/6 indication.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ pembrolizumab * msi_high + age + ecog + stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.180, p=0.29. Direction consistent but not significant; MSI-high is rare (n=498).

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ sacituzumab_govitecan * triple_neg + age + ecog + stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.033, p=0.52. No differential benefit in triple-negative subset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.100, p=9.4e-112. Higher albumin associated with longer PFS as predicted.
Pearson r=-0.116, p=4.1e-148. Higher weight loss associated with shorter PFS as predicted.
Pearson r=-0.016, p=2.5e-4. Small but significant negative association.
Pearson r=+0.003, p=0.54. No association detected.
Pearson r=-0.003, p=0.46. No association detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
has_brain_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
liver_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of stage and ECOG.
bone_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of stage and ECOG.
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + liver_mets + bone_mets + weight_loss_pct_6mo + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + nlr + age_years').fit()
analyses.result_summary: ECOG -1.165 per unit (p<1e-300); stage_iv -1.550 (p<1e-300); has_brain_mets -0.989 (p<1e-300); weight_loss -0.077/% (p<1e-300); albumin +0.481 (p<1e-300); LDH -0.0006/U (p=5.8e-14); liver_mets, bone_mets, nlr not independently significant. Notably age_years has a POSITIVE coefficient +0.176 per year (p<1e-300), opposite of expected.
ECOG=0 mean PFS=5.640 (n=17592); ECOG=1 mean PFS=4.444 (n=24971); ECOG=2 mean PFS=3.289 (n=7437). Monotonic decline.

analyses.code: coefficient on age_years in adjusted OLS
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age coef=+0.176 per year (p<1e-300) — REFUTES h5.6: older patients in this cohort have longer PFS, contrary to typical clinical expectation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity groups (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (medicare, medicaid, private, uninsured).
rural_residence=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than urban residence.
After adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, and brain mets, race_ethnicity is independently associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA F=0.85, p=0.49 across 5 groups; means range 4.679 (white) to 4.762 (other). No significant differences.
ANOVA F=0.31, p=0.82; means 4.685 (private) to 4.733 (uninsured). No differences detected.
Rural mean 4.714 (n=9996) vs urban 4.688; diff +0.027, p=0.34. No significant disparity in either direction.

analyses.code: ols with race dummies + insurance dummies + rural + age + ecog + stage_iv + brain_mets
analyses.result_summary: All race and insurance dummy coefficients have p>0.3 after adjustment; rural_residence coef +0.030, p=0.054 borderline. Refutes h6.4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 24 candidate SNPs (snp_rs*), at least one is associated with pfs_months at a Bonferroni-corrected significance level (p < 0.05/24 = 0.00208).
snp_rs1801131 (the most significant candidate by uncorrected p-value, r=-0.010, p=0.026) is associated with pfs_months at the conventional 0.05 threshold without correction.

analyses.code: for s in snp_cols: pearsonr(df[s], df.pfs_months); count p<0.00208
analyses.result_summary: Screened 24 SNPs against pfs_months. Minimum p-value = 0.026 (snp_rs1801131). 0 SNPs pass Bonferroni threshold of 0.00208. Refutes h7.1.
Pearson r=-0.0099, p=0.026 — nominally significant uncorrected, but the effect size is tiny (~0.01 SD) and this is one of 24 tests so not credible.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS effect of treatment_tamoxifen differs between black-race patients and others (treatment_tamoxifen x black-race interaction on pfs_months).
The PFS effect of treatment_palbociclib differs between black-race patients and others.
The PFS effect of treatment_pembrolizumab differs between black-race patients and others.
The PFS effect of treatment_olaparib differs between black-race patients and others.
Treatment receipt (proportion receiving each agent) differs by race_ethnicity.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ tamoxifen * black + age + ecog + stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Interaction +0.050, p=0.25. No evidence of differential effect.
Interaction +0.016, p=0.68. No evidence of differential effect.
Interaction -0.029, p=0.60. No differential effect.
Interaction -0.063, p=0.34. No differential effect.

analyses.code: groupby race_ethnicity, mean of each treatment column
analyses.result_summary: Treatment receipt is essentially uniform across race groups (e.g., palbociclib 0.347-0.352; trastuzumab 0.189-0.203; pembrolizumab 0.145-0.161). No clinically meaningful disparities. Refutes h8.5.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, autoimmune_disease, prior_malignancy) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher symptom grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
After adjusting for age, ECOG, and stage, at least one comorbidity remains an independent predictor of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: All 12 comorbidities tested individually have |diff|<0.05 months and p>0.15. None are significantly associated with PFS unadjusted. Refutes h9.1.

analyses.code: pearsonr for each symptom grade vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All five symptom grades have |r|<0.005 and p>0.29 against PFS. Refutes h9.2 — symptom grades carry no PFS signal in this cohort.

analyses.code: ols pfs ~ comorbs + symptoms + age + ecog + stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Only dyspnea_grade reaches nominal significance (coef +0.011, p=0.048) but with positive sign opposite to expected. No comorbidity is independently associated. Effectively refutes h9.3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib's PFS benefit is reduced (negative interaction) in pik3ca_mutation-positive patients compared with PIK3CA wild-type.
treatment_pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months more in triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-) patients than in others.
Among BRCA1/2 wild-type patients, treatment_olaparib does NOT prolong pfs_months (i.e., olaparib effect is restricted to BRCA carriers — negative olaparib x no_brca interaction).
treatment_trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months more in HER2-low patients than in HER2-low-negative patients (trastuzumab x her2_low positive interaction).
treatment_palbociclib prolongs pfs_months more in postmenopausal patients than in premenopausal patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab is less effective (more negative) in patients with has_brain_mets=1 than without.
Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0 patients in this 'breast cancer' cohort (which is only 44.7% female, suggesting the cohort is not exclusively breast cancer).

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ palbociclib * pik3ca_mutation + er_positive + age + ecog
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -1.663, p<1e-300. Strongly supported: palbociclib coef = +1.676 in PIK3CA-wt patients, but the +1.676 benefit is essentially erased (-1.663) when PIK3CA mutated. PIK3CA mutation appears to abrogate palbociclib's benefit in this dataset.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ pembrolizumab * triple_neg + age + ecog + stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.073, p=0.092. Trend in the predicted direction but not significant. In non-TNBC patients pembrolizumab coef is -0.055 (p=0.006), so on net the small benefit in TNBC nearly offsets the negative effect elsewhere.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ olaparib * no_brca + age + ecog (no_brca = 1 - brca_any)
analyses.result_summary: olaparib coef in BRCA carriers = +0.152 (p=0.16); olaparib x no_brca interaction = -0.192 (p=0.083). Trend supports h10.3 (olaparib benefit restricted to BRCA), though neither term is independently significant.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ trastuzumab * her2_low + age + ecog
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.008, p=0.84. No evidence of differential trastuzumab benefit in HER2-low.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ palbociclib * postmenopausal + er_positive + age
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.069, p=0.033 — small but significant NEGATIVE interaction; palbociclib's PFS gain is slightly LESS in postmenopausal patients, opposite of the proposed direction. Refutes h10.5.

analyses.code: ols pfs_months ~ pembrolizumab * has_brain_mets + age + ecog + stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.032, p=0.58. No evidence of differential effect by brain mets status.

analyses.code: ttest by sex_female; group means of treatments by sex
analyses.result_summary: PFS mean 4.686 (female) vs 4.699 (male); diff -0.013, p=0.56. Treatment receipt is essentially identical between sexes (palbociclib 0.348 F vs 0.349 M, etc.). The cohort is labeled 'breast' but is 44.7% female and shows no sex-based clinical structure consistent with breast-cancer-only population.
